items.verse: وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ لِمَا تَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُكُمُ ٱلْكَذِبَ هَـٰذَا حَلَـٰلٌۭ وَهَـٰذَا حَرَامٌۭ لِّتَفْتَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ
items.verseEn: And do not say about what your tongues assert of untruth, "This is lawful and this is unlawful," to invent falsehood about Allāh. Indeed, those who invent falsehood about Allāh will not succeed.

items.verse: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَوْ كَانَ خَيْرًۭا مَّا سَبَقُونَآ إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَإِذْ لَمْ يَهْتَدُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هَـٰذَآ إِفْكٌۭ قَدِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And those who disbelieve say of those who believe, "If it had [truly] been good, they would not have preceded us to it." And when they are not guided by it, they will say, "This is an ancient falsehood."

items.verse: ٱلَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ ٱللَّهَ قِيَـٰمًۭا وَقُعُودًۭا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَـٰذَا بَـٰطِلًۭا سُبْحَـٰنَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ
items.verseEn: Who remember Allāh while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides and give thought to the creation of the heavens and the earth, [saying], "Our Lord, You did not create this aimlessly; exalted are You [above such a thing]; then protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
And they used to persist in the great violation,

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ قِصَّةِ مُوَاجَهَةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لِعَبَدَةِ الْأَصْنَامِ
categories.nameEn: Words from the story of Prophet Ibrahim's confrontation with the idolaters

items.verse: أُفٍّۢ لَّكُمْ وَلِمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
items.verseEn: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allāh. Then will you not use reason?"

items.verse: فَجَعَلَهُمْ جُذَٰذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرًۭا لَّهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ يَرْجِعُونَ
items.verseEn: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it [and question].

items.verse: فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ ءَالِهَتِهِمْ فَقَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ
items.verseEn: Then he turned to their gods and said, "Do you not eat?

items.verse: قُلْنَا يَـٰنَارُ كُونِى بَرْدًۭا وَسَلَـٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ
items.verseEn: We [i.e., Allāh] said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham."

items.verse: قَالَ مَا خَطْبُكُنَّ إِذْ رَٰوَدتُّنَّ يُوسُفَ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ ۚ قُلْنَ حَـٰشَ لِلَّهِ مَا عَلِمْنَا عَلَيْهِ مِن سُوٓءٍۢ ۚ قَالَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْـَٔـٰنَ حَصْحَصَ ٱلْحَقُّ أَنَا۠ رَٰوَدتُّهُۥ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ
items.verseEn: Said [the king to the women], "What was your condition when you sought to seduce Joseph?" They said, "Perfect is Allāh! We know about him no evil." The wife of al-ʿAzeez said, "Now the truth has become evident. It was I who sought to seduce him, and indeed, he is of the truthful.

items.verse: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِذَنۢبِهِمْ فَسَوَّىٰهَا
items.verseEn: But they denied him and hamstrung her. So their Lord brought down upon them destruction for their sin and made it equal [upon all of them].
So they will be overturned into it [i.e., Hellfire], they and the deviators
And by the night as it closes in

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَخْتِمُ سُوَرَهَا
categories.nameEn: Words that are the final word of their respective Surah

items.verseEn: From among the jinn and mankind".
And from the evil of an envier when he envies."
Around her neck is a rope of [twisted] fiber.
For you is your religion, and for me is my religion."

harf.hint: It belongs to Allah alone; a divine ruling or decree.
harf.arabicVerse: ذَٰلِكُم بِأَنَّهُۥٓ إِذَا دُعِىَ ٱللَّهُ وَحْدَهُۥ كَفَرْتُمْ ۖ وَإِن يُشْرَكْ بِهِۦ تُؤْمِنُوا۟ ۚ فَٱلْحُكْمُ لِلَّهِ ٱلْعَلِىِّ ٱلْكَبِيرِ
harf.verse: 40:12 — [They will be told], "That is because, when Allāh was called upon alone, you disbelieved; but if others were associated with Him, you believed. So the judgement is with Allāh, the Most High, the Grand."

deduction.title: The Guardian of a Sacred Trust
deduction.intro: In the quiet halls of a sacred sanctuary, a devoted servant of Allah carried a heavy heart. With age advancing and no heir to carry on the legacy, this pious soul turned to the Divine in a moment of profound and private supplication. What followed was a miracle that defied the laws of nature, a testament to unwavering faith. Your task is to uncover the identity of this remarkable figure.
clues: I was entrusted with the care of a pious young individual who was destined for greatness, a sign for all people.
Whenever I entered the sanctuary to see the one in my care, I found provisions that were not from me, a miracle from Allah.
Despite my old age and my wife's barrenness, I secretly called out to my Lord for a righteous heir.
While I was standing in prayer in the sanctuary, angels brought me the glad tidings of a son.
As a sign from my Lord, I was commanded not to speak to people for three nights, though I was perfectly able.
The son I was granted was named Yahya, a name not given to anyone before him.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Zakariyya
'Imran
Prophet 'Isa
Prophet Yahya
Luqman the Wise

trial.label: What Was My Core Supplication?
options: A prayer for a righteous heir
A request for a kingdom like no other
A plea to be saved from a great flood
A prayer for forgiveness after being swallowed by a large fish
A request for a heavenly feast
trial.answer: A prayer for a righteous heir

location.label: What Was The Sign Given To Me?
options: The inability to speak for three days
A staff that turned into a serpent
A hand that shone white without disease
A camel emerging from a rock
A cool and peaceful fire
location.answer: The inability to speak for three days

outcome.label: What Was The Miraculous Result?
options: The birth of my son, Yahya
The parting of the Red Sea
The construction of a great ship
The submission of the wind and jinn
The virgin birth of a child to the one in my care
outcome.answer: The birth of my son, Yahya

deduction.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱجْعَل لِّىٓ ءَايَةًۭ ۚ قَالَ ءَايَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ ٱلنَّاسَ ثَلَـٰثَ لَيَالٍۢ سَوِيًّۭا
deduction.verse: [Zechariah] said, "My Lord, make for me a sign." He said, "Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, [being] sound." (19:10)

translations: And said your Lord Call upon Me I will respond
scramble.hint: A powerful promise from Allah regarding supplication (du'a) and His immediate response.
scramble.arabic: وَقَالَ رَبُّكُمُ ٱدْعُونِىٓ أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِى سَيَدْخُلُونَ جَهَنَّمَ دَاخِرِينَ
scramble.verseEn: And your Lord says, "Call upon Me; I will respond to you." Indeed, those who disdain My worship will enter Hell [rendered] contemptible.

verse.arabic_text: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍۢ وَأُنثَىٰ وَجَعَلْنَـٰكُمْ شُعُوبًۭا وَقَبَآئِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ أَتْقَىٰكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌۭ
verse.translation: O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you. Indeed, Allah is Knowing and Acquainted.

theme_question.correct: The depth of their piety and God-consciousness (Taqwa).
options: The extent of their wealth and possessions.
The prestige of their family lineage and tribe.
The depth of their piety and God-consciousness (Taqwa).
The vastness of their worldly knowledge and education.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse was revealed to address issues of tribalism and racism. It establishes a universal principle that supersedes all worldly standards of status. Some narrations link it to an incident involving Bilal ibn Rabah (RA), emphasizing that his status as a former Abyssinian slave was irrelevant in the sight of Allah compared to his profound piety.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is the abolition of false pride based on race, ethnicity, or social status. Allah explicitly states that He created human diversity not for division or hierarchy, but for mutual recognition ('lita'arafu'). The sole criterion for honor ('akramakum') before Him is 'Taqwa'—piety, righteousness, and God-consciousness. This verse is a foundational charter for equality and human dignity in Islam.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Hujurat (The Rooms) is a Madani surah often called the 'Surah of Manners' (Akhlaq). It provides crucial guidance on the etiquette a believer should have towards Allah, His Messenger (ﷺ), and fellow Muslims. It addresses issues like raising one's voice, verifying news, and avoiding mockery, suspicion, and backbiting. The surah culminates with this powerful verse (49:13) on human unity and the true basis of honor.
